name: Kebro (sistrum / rattle)
description: A handheld percussion instrument used in Ethiopian Orthodox worship to mark rhythm and solemnity.
useContext: Often during processions and certain hymnody; parish customs vary.
howToHold: Hold the handle without gripping tightly; let the plates speak with small, controlled motion.
movementBasics: Coordinate with the cantor’s tempo; avoid overpowering the human voice.
mistakesToAvoid: Over-shaking, showy gestures, or treating it as performance rather than service.

name: Tsenatsil (bow / prostration)
description: Not an object you hold — a reverent movement of the body toward God and the sacred.
useContext: At the name of the Lord, gospel, or as parish custom directs.
howToHold: N/A — from the waist or full prostration per guidance.
movementBasics: Brief, reverent, and repeatable; breathe out as you bow; rise with calm.
mistakesToAvoid: Deep prostration without blessing or health to do so; speed that looks restless.

name: Mequamia (staff / prayer stick)
description: A wooden prayer staff used in standing prayer and procession; meaning is reverence and support.
useContext: Standing prayer, liturgical processions; some homes use a simple cross or none.
howToHold: Upright or angled gently; follow the form your parish teaches.
movementBasics: Let the staff rest rather than wave; steps stay measured with the choir.
mistakesToAvoid: Leaning all your weight so it becomes a prop for posture collapse.
